preSessionState.CLAUDE.md:
# Project Guidelines

## Architecture
- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
- All business logic lives in the service layer

## Coding Conventions
- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
- Prefer async/await over raw Promises

## Testing
- Tests use vitest
- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method

## File Organization
- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
- src/services/ — Business logic layer
- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
- src/config/ — Configuration files
- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure

## Git Practices
- Commit atomically per logical change
- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
- Run tests before committing

{"entry_type":"decision","summary":"Unified notification system on event-driven EventBus pattern with pluggable adapter routing","detail":"EventBus is the right choice because: (1) the codebase already describes itself as event-driven in package.json and index.ts, (2) EventBus supports async handlers, event logging, and replay — CallbackRegistry does not, (3) EventBus provides better decoupling — emitters don't know about listeners, (4) the typed event system (AppEvent union) provides compile-time safety. The adapter pattern on top allows adding new channels (webhook, SMS, in-app) without modifying the service.","tags":["architecture"],"scope":"src/services/notification.service.ts","agent_id":"agent-1","id":"01KN0WDYBW2BW1PW4Z5EYKA5CZ","timestamp":"2026-03-31T02:44:12.284Z"}
{"entry_type":"status","summary":"Agent 1 complete: notification system foundation implemented with EventBus + adapter pattern","detail":"Implemented notification system foundation:

**Architecture**: Event-driven via EventBus with pluggable NotificationAdapter routing.

**Files created**:
- `src/models/notification.ts` — Core types: NotificationChannel, NotificationType, NotificationPayload, NotificationRecord, NotificationResult
- `src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts` — NotificationAdapter interface (all channels must implement)
- `src/adapters/email.adapter.ts` — EmailAdapter implementing NotificationAdapter for 'email' channel

**Files modified**:
- `src/services/notification.service.ts` — Rewritten: subscribes to EventBus events, translates to NotificationPayload, routes to registered adapters
- `src/services/order.service.ts` — Migrated order:status-changed from CallbackRegistry to EventBus
- `src/index.ts` — Added exports for new types and adapters
- `tests/services/notification.service.test.ts` — Updated for new API (NotificationRecord shape, EventBus-only pattern, adapter tests)
- `tests/services/order.service.test.ts` — Updated assertion for status-changed now going through EventBus

**Constraints for other agents**:
1. All notifications MUST go through EventBus.emit()
2. All delivery channels MUST implement NotificationAdapter interface
3. Register adapters via notificationService.registerAdapter()
4. New triggers need: event type in event-types.ts + handler in NotificationService + emit in originating service
